fragment_01.fact_card: A small gear etched with an old workshop mark. The teeth still mesh cleanly, suggesting it belonged to a precision timepiece rather than a farm tool.

fragment_02.fact_card: A curved brass plate with faint engraving worn by handling. The patina pattern implies decades inside a closed case rather than open air.

fragment_03.fact_card: A glass crystal, lightly clouded at the edges. Its diameter matches the plate and gear, confirming these pieces once shared the same face.

fragment_04.fact_card: A coiled mainspring, still retaining tension. The alloy and finish are consistent with local repair-workshop stock from the 1920s.

fragment_05.fact_card: A final bridge piece that joins the gear train to the escapement. With this fragment, the five pieces can be aligned into a single movement.
